title: mathewsia auriculata
intro: Mathewsia auriculata, also known as the Mexican honey flower, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Mathewsia auriculata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of colds and coughs.
